reviews.review_title: The better of the Pickleball VR apps...
reviews.review_description:
I have had a chance to try "Pickleball VR" and compare them with the other apps.  And for now as an AppLab Pickleball trainer (which is still under development and not finalized), it has a lot of potential but there are a few things I hope developers consider in forthcoming updates (which I am hoping there are plans for updates).

a) With most training simulators, there is a video or instructions that make it easier for different players to understand on what to do.  This would be good to see that.
b) An easy selection menu of quitting/restarting while pausing would be nice.
c) While the better of the VR Pickleball games in terms of physics, there is always room for improvement, especially in serving.  Certain mid-strength hits go far over the fence, certain heavy-strength hits are mid.
d) While I like the ball sounds and bird chirps, custom music accessibility would be a great option, as some will use this trainer as a work out.
e) Wall Mode - Heading to the cold season, would be great to have a wall mode to hit balls freely.  
f) Quick Reaction/Return Mode; Volley mode, Volley return mode
g)  Mini Games - Hit bowling pins, cones, balloons, targets, etc.  (similar to tennis game training modes/mini-games)
h) Achievement mode - Earn achievements, utilize different racquet faces/colors
i)  Different venues and locations, scenery
j) Now this is just more of a dream request but similar to a popular table tennis game on VR, a ghost opponent or bot would be great. I know this is an app more for training, but to have a ghost opponent would be nice.

There is no doubt that "Pickleball VR" has a lot of potential.  Considering it's made from a bigger company, compared to the other games, this could really be something much bigger and better if they want.  Hopefully more updates are forthcoming. I like what I see so far! 

Also, it's unusual but this app shows up on the mobile version of the store app, but not on the desktop version when keyword, "pickleball" is searched.

The physics need a lot of attention - currently they’re unrealistic and there’s no purpose to the app besides wanting to hit something somewhere. Once it’s overhauled, it could be a great practice app.

I play pickleball regularly at a 4.0 level and this game is nowhere ready. Not accurate at all. You could not use this to get better or improve anything. Great idea, just not there yet. Keep at it though.

Thank you for developing this game. I love the fact that play is on a full court. However…. 
1) the net is too high (not regulation)
2) The lobster machine setting should be adjustable. 
3) The serves keep goi g to the left. I normally hit my servers in the box and to the baseline. It is very difficult to do this with this game. 
4) Dinking should be done at the non volley zone. Not midcourt which is where the balls served by the lobster machine force you to stand. 
Good luck with this app. I will be watching to see how it improves!
Nice graphics. That’s it. Physics are awful. Save your hard earned money and avoid this app.
This game is awful, save your money.

At the moment, at least, you can only practice. You can't actually play against a person or the computer.

Also, the physics are completely unpredictable. If you land a shot, it's basically just luck.

….but only *that just now. I have great expectations; I’m a regular PB player, (3.75 indoors and out; looking to 4.0 by April).
There seems zero reaction to spin so far, which may be due to the limited camera view in Q2: it cannot see behind you. (Quest Pro controllers can)

However, all dinks appear to have topspin, as they jet upon bouncingand are nearly impossible to play, compared to real play.

Swings that DO connect have very little relationship with real play. They can be short or over the fence.

There’s no adjustment of hand placement/grip yet, which needs consideration —like in Eleven.
Waaay too many edge mis-shots, as paddle position subtlety appears elusive yet.

In the training, slams on a bounce should count as a hit, as targets include unavoidable outofbounds, and foot shots are harder than slams, anyway.
Position movement needs to be faster.
Haptics should be invoked for dynamics, at least *some indication of power.
On serves, Ball *could just appear in the non dominant hand?
Not a single service has *felt right, many overshot.

Look forward to the next developments!
———
Update 3/4/23:
Doesn’t seem to be much change, if any, since downloading. 
Very difficult to control, though several hours ingame, more control is gained.
Serving is unrealistic; returns equally-so. 
Will update.
The worst Pickleball app out there lol I know I’ve tried them all

The graphics are fine but nothing else. The drills (which is all you get) suck and are unrealistic.  You cannot play another player. Every drill practice is not realistic and requires no hand/eye coordination.

reviews.review_title: Great Visual Design - But Needs Work
reviews.review_description:
Overall a good proof of concept, but needs more fleshing out to be a great game / drill instructor.

Pros:
+ Great visual design (consistent, quality graphics - drills are clear on what you need to do)
+ Decent sound design

Needs Improvement:
- Physics and mechanics behaves unexpectedly, even after a long period of testing, I had trouble adapting.
- Singles and Doubles would be wonderful.

This game thinks I'm about 4 ft tall. I'm looking forward to updates as I can see it has the potential to be good for drills. I would really like it if it were good for drills. As is it is not worth the money :-(
